expectations.text: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: All 9 patterns found: ## Problem (L12), ## Solution (L17), ## Scope (L20), ## Acceptance Criteria (L41), ## Design Decisions (L52), ### Failure Modes (L70), ## Risks & Open Questions (L77, matches '## Risks'), ## Alternatives Considered (L83, matches '## Alternatives'), ## Verification (L89).

expectations.text: No code fences (```) anywhere in the spec outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Scanned all 128 lines. No triple-backtick code fences found anywhere. Inline single-backtick code used on L100-102 for commands, but no ``` code fence blocks exist.

expectations.text: No TypeScript interface/type definitions in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions section (L52-76) contains no 'interface ', 'type ' declarations, ': string', ': number', ': boolean', or '=> Promise'. References like 'confirmationRequired' (L64) and 'success:false' (L75) describe runtime values in prose, not TypeScript type syntax.

expectations.text: No JSON object literals or proto field listings in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L52-76) describes data in prose: 'dashboard title and UID, the active tab, current time range, template variable names and their current values, and a per-panel digest (ID, title, type, a short description...)' (L54). No { key: value } JSON shapes, no field-list structures, no data structure definitions.

expectations.text: Scope items describe capabilities/behaviors, not exact file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope section (L24-33) contains 9 items, all behavioral: 'Provide dashboard context to the LLM...', 'Navigate between dashboard tabs...', 'Change the dashboard time range override via chat', etc. No 'src/features/...' paths, no '.ts' or '.tsx' extensions.

expectations.text: Spec is under 120 lines (excluding Implementation Notes appendix)
expectations.evidence: Spec is 128 lines total. There is no section named 'Implementation Notes' to exclude. The spec has '## Implementation Delta' (L120) and '## AGENTS.md Updates' (L125), but neither is 'Implementation Notes'. Without an Implementation Notes section to exclude, the full 128 lines count, exceeding the 120-line limit.

expectations.text: Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold topic sentences that state decisions as facts
expectations.evidence: All 8 Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold sentences: L54 '**Context is a point-in-time summary...**', L56 '**Panel summaries include query descriptions...**', L58 '**Tab navigation uses the existing hash-based routing...**', L60 '**Template variable changes go through the same local state setter...**', L62 '**Fullscreen toggle identifies panels by title...**', L64 '**Edit mode and save are confirmation-gated...**', L66 '**Export returns the dashboard model JSON...**', L68 '**Both hooks are called unconditionally...**'. 8/8 = 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: An implementer could start coding from this spec without asking questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names the pattern to follow (three-step chat integration from chat AGENTS.md, L18), state sources (useDashboard for server state, useDashboardViewStore for view state, L54), each tool's behavior and validation logic (L58-66), hook ordering requirement (L68), stale-state solution via useLatestRef (L72), confirmation requirements (L64), context serialization approach with 4KB budget (L54-56), and disambiguation strategy for panel matching (L62). All core tools have defined behavior, error handling, and state coordination.

expectations.text: Design Decisions section contains decisions, not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L52-76) contains: (1) no code snippets/fences, (2) no TS interface/type definitions, (3) no JSON shapes, (4) no exact line numbers, (5) no feature-to-handler mapping tables, (6) references to existing hooks like 'useState' (L60) and 'useLatestRef' (L72) are architectural references to existing code (consistent with 'naming modules is OK'), not new implementation definitions, (7) no new file paths. All content is prose describing decisions and their rationale.

expectations.text: Failure modes state policy decisions, not just error types
expectations.evidence: 4 failure modes (L72-75): (1) Stale state → use useLatestRef wrappers (technical pattern choice, borderline). (2) Variable name doesn't exist → validate and return error listing available variables, do NOT silently create (explicit policy against silent creation). (3) Ambiguous panel title → return all matches and ask user to clarify, rather than picking arbitrarily (disambiguation policy). (4) Save fails → return error, do NOT retry automatically (no-retry policy). 3 of 4 (75%) are clear policy decisions with stated alternatives. >50% threshold met.

expectations.text: Scope items describe capabilities and behaviors, not files or architecture
expectations.evidence: All 9 In Scope items (L24-33) are behavioral: 'Provide dashboard context to the LLM including...', 'Navigate between dashboard tabs...via chat', 'Change the dashboard time range override via chat', 'Toggle fullscreen on a specific panel by name or ID via chat', 'Switch into and out of edit mode via chat', 'Change template variable values via chat', 'Export the dashboard JSON via chat', 'Save the dashboard via chat', 'Register suggested prompts for the dashboard page'. Zero items name specific files, hooks, or components. 9/9 = 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: Spec has a well-formed Acceptance Criteria section with behavioral 'done' conditions
expectations.evidence: ## Acceptance Criteria exists at L41. Contains exactly 8 checkbox items (L43-50), within the 4-8 range. All items are behavioral/observable outcomes: chat FAB appears/disappears (L43), context-based answer without API calls (L44), tab navigation with UI reflection (L45), fullscreen activation by title (L46), confirmation card for edit mode (L47), template variable update with query refresh (L48), export returns serialized data (L49), save with confirmation (L50). Items cover all core prompt behaviors: panel context, tab navigation, time ranges (addressed in scope/design, not a separate AC — but context-based questions cover it), fullscreen, edit mode + confirmation, template variables, export, save + confirmation.

expectations.text: Verification section includes agent-actionable checks with action → outcome format
expectations.evidence: ### Agent-Verifiable subsection exists at L106. Contains 6 checks (L108-113), all using 'Inspect X → verify Y' action-outcome format: (1) 'Inspect useDashboardChatContext → verify it calls setContextGetter on mount and clearContextGetter on unmount', (2) 'Inspect useDashboardTools → verify all mutating tools have confirmationRequired set to true', (3) 'Inspect Dashboard.tsx → verify both chat hooks are called before any early return', (4) 'Inspect tool handlers → verify every handler uses useLatestRef', (5) 'Inspect context getter → verify serialized output includes panel titles, types...', (6) 'Inspect suggested prompts → verify dashboard key is registered'. All are concrete code inspection tasks executable by an agent with code tools.
